title: Receiver and decoding method thereof
abstract: A receiver is provided, which includes an antenna configured to receive a plurality of superposition coded signals from a plurality of base stations; and a signal processor configured to perform decoding of a plurality of layer signals included in the superposition coded signals by decoding an uppermost layer signal among the layer signals through applying Gaussian approximation only to remaining layer signals except an upper layer signal which is a next uppermost layer signal among the layer signals.
claims: Claims ( 14 ) A receiver comprising: an antenna configured to receive a plurality of superposition coded signals from a plurality of base stations; and a signal processor configured to perform decoding of a plurality of layer signals included in the superposition coded signals by decoding an uppermost layer signal among the layer signals through applying Gaussian approximation only to remaining layer signals except an upper layer signal which is a next uppermost layer signal among the layer signals. The receiver of claim 1, wherein the signal processor processes the upper layer signal as received from at least one of the base stations as an interference signal without performing the Gaussian approximation to the upper layer signal. The receiver of claim 2, wherein the signal processor further performs decoding of the upper layer signal through applying the Gaussian approximation to the remaining layer signals except the upper layer signal. The receiver of claim 3, wherein the signal processor calculates a Log Likelihood Ratio (LLR) of the upper layer signal based on a signal which is obtained by cancelling the decoded uppermost layer signal from the superposition coded signals, and decodes the upper layer signal based on the LLR of the upper layer signal. The receiver of claim 4, wherein the signal processor further performs decoding of the remaining signals except the upper layer signal. The receiver as claimed in claim 1, wherein the uppermost layer signal and the upper layer signal are signals which are modulated according to a QPSK method. The receiver as claimed in claim 6, wherein the signal processor calculates a Log Likelihood Ratio (LLR) of the uppermost layer signal based on a following equation, , where y denotes the superposition coded signals received from the base stations, x 1 denotes the uppermost layer signal, and x 2 denotes the upper layer signal, and wherein the signal processor performs the decoding of the uppermost signal based on the LLR. A method of decoding a plurality of superposition coded signals at a receiver, the method comprising: receiving the plurality of superposition coded signals from a plurality of base stations; and performing decoding of a plurality of layer signals included in the superposition coded signals by decoding an uppermost layer signal among the layer signals through applying Gaussian approximation only to remaining layer signals except an upper layer signal which is a next uppermost layer signal among the layer signals. The method of claim 8, wherein the decoding the uppermost layer signal comprises processing the upper layer signal as received from at least one of the base stations as an interference signal without performing the Gaussian approximation to the upper layer signal. The method of claim 9, further comprising decoding the upper layer signal through applying the Gaussian approximation to the remaining layer signals except the upper layer signal. The method of claim 10, wherein the decoding the upper layer signal comprises calculating a Log Likelihood Ratio (LLR) of the upper layer signal based on a signal which is obtained by cancelling the decoded uppermost layer signal from the superposition coded signals, and decoding the upper layer signal based on the LLR of the upper layer signal. The method of claim 11, further comprising decoding the remaining signals except the upper layer signal. The method of claim 8, wherein the uppermost layer signal and the upper layer signal are signals which are modulated according to a QPSK method. The method of claim 13, wherein the decoding the uppermost layer signal comprises: calculating a Log Likelihood Ratio (LLR) for decoding the uppermost layer signal based on a following equation, , where y denotes the superposition coded signals received from the base stations, x 1 denotes the uppermost layer signal, and x 2 denotes the upper layer signal; and decoding the uppermost signal based on the LLR.
